IRS990.ActivityOrMissionDesc: TO PROVIDE HEALTH CARE SERVICES AND EDUCATION TO NATIVE AMERICANS.
IRS990.MissionDesc: TO WORK TOGETHER WITH OUR CLIENTS AND COMMUNITY TO ACHIEVE WELLNESS THROUGH HEALTH SERVICES THAT REFLECT THE TRADITIONAL VALUES OF OUR AMERICAN INDIAN COMMUNITY.
IRS990.Desc: THE ORGANIZATION PROVIDED MEDICAL, DENTAL, VISION, MENTAL HEALTH, HOSPITAL, PHARMACY, PHYSICAL AND OCCUPATIONAL THERAPY, AND OTHER HEALTH SERVICES TO NATIVE AMERICANS AND THEIR FAMILIES. TOTAL VISITS DURING THE YEAR 2023-2024 WERE 60,526. OF THE TOTAL 5,813 WERE FOR NON-INDIANS (Apx. 10%).ADDITIONALLY, THE ORGANIZATION PROVIDED DIABETES TREATMENT, EDUCATION,AND PREVENTION HEALTH SERVICES. NUTRITION AND OTHER SOCIAL SERVICE PROGRAMS FOR LOW INCOME NATIVE AMERICANS (WIC, TITLE VI, ETC.). THEY ALSO PROVIDED TOBACCO EDUCATION TO NATIVE AMERICANS IN HUMBOLDT AND DEL NORTE COUNTY. THE PROGRAM PROVIDES MATERIALS ON THE HAZARDS OF TOBACCO USE, PEER-LED PREVENTION PRESENTATIONS, AND COUNSELING SERVICES.WE BECAME MEMBERS OF DRUG-MEDICAL FOR 2023-2024 ALLOWING FOR BILLING AND EXPANSION OF OUR BEHAVIORAL HEALTH DEPARTMENT. THIS SERVICE EXPANDS BEYOND OUR SUICIDE, TOBACCO AND OPIOID COUNSELING TO INCLUDE THERAPEUTIC SERVICES FOR ALL AGES.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Part II-B, Line 1i - Other Activities Description
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: The Darrel Hostler Fund provides the resources for Califronia Rural Indian Health Board to provide legislation and advocacy to the Indians of California. Activities performed include lobbying, travel to Washington DC and attending events specifically for advocacy.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Part II, Line 9: Organization Reporting of conservation easements.
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: EXPENSES RELATED TO THE CONSERVATION EASEMENT ARE REPORTED BY THE ORGANIZATION IN THEIR NATURAL EXPENSE CATEGORIES. NO SEPARATE BALANCE SHEET AMOUNT OR FOOTNOTE DISCLOSURE HAS BEEN REPORTED.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Part III, Line 1a: If organization elected under SFAS 116 to not report are, historical treasures, o
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE ART COLLECTION HELD BY UNITED INDIAN HEALTH SERVICES IS COMPRISED OF SAMPLES OF ORIGINAL ARTWORKS BY PROMINENT NORTHWEST ARTISTS AND ARTWORKS OF LESSER KNOWN ARTISTS FROM OUR REGION.THE COLLECTION IS UTILIZED TO MAKE THE INDIAN COMMUNITY COME TOGETHER AS A WHOLE, TO IMPROVE THE OVERALL HEALTH AND WELLNESS OF EVERYONE INVOLVED, AND TO BRING THE INDIAN AND NON-INDIAN COMMUNITIES CLOSER TOGETHER.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Section A, Line 2
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: BOARD MEMBER FAMILY RELATIONSHIPS:1. LAWANDA GREEN (CHAIRPERSON) AND JOHN GREEN (ALTERNATE)2. RUBY ROLLINGS (ALTERNATE) AND SHIRLEY LAOS (MEMBER AT LARGE)3. LAURA BORDEN (BOARD MEMBER) AND LANA MCCOVEY (BOARD MEMBER)

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Section A, Line 6
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: MEMBERSHIP IN UIHS IS DEFINED IN THE ARTICLES OF INCORPORATION. ELIGIBLE INDIAN BENEFICIARIES WHO MEET CERTAIN REQUIREMENTS ARE ELIGIBLE TO BE MEMBERS.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Section B, Line 11b
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE TAX RETURN IS SENT TO THE ENTIRE BOARD OF DIRECTORS FOR REVIEW PRIOR TO FILING AND SIGNED BY THE CFO.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Section B, Line 12c
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: ANY DIRECTOR WHO HAS ANY PROPRIETARY INTEREST IN, OR WHO RECEIVES OR HAS EXPECTATIONS OF RECEIVING COMPENSATION FROM OR WHO WILL HAVE ANY SIGNIFICANT MONETARY GAIN OR LOSS AS A RESULT OF THE CORPORATION'S ACTIONS OR ANY ORGANIZATIONS, PROJECT, PROGRAM, CONTRACT, OR OTHER ACTION UNDER CONSIDERATION BY THE CORPORATION OR ITS BOARD, SHALL BE DEEMED TO HAVE A CONFLICT OF INTEREST.WHENEVER ANY DIRECTOR HAS OR HAS CAUSE TO BELIEVE THAT AN ACTION OR ISSUE PENDING BEFORE THE BOARD OR THE CORPORATION COULD INVOLVE A CONFLICT OF INTEREST FOR A BOARD MEMBER, HE OR SHE SHALL ANNOUNCE THE CONFLICT AND SHALL THEN ABSTAIN FROM DISCUSSION, CONSIDERATION, DECISIONS, AND VOTING ON THE MATTER.WHENEVER ANY DIRECTOR SITS ON THE GOVERNING BODY OF A TRIBE, ORGANIZATION, PROGRAM, OR OTHER PROJECT WHICH IS COMPETING FOR FUNDING WITH THE CORPORATION, HE OR SHE SHALL ANNOUNCE THE CONFLICT OF INTEREST AND SO NOTIFY THE CHAIRMAN OF THE BOARD. SUCH PERSON SHALL THEN ABSTAIN FROM DISCUSSING, CONSIDERING, DECIDING, AND VOTING ON THAT FUNDING MATTER.NO DIRECTOR MAY BE AN EMPLOYEE OF THE CORPORATION DURING THE DIRECTOR'S TERM OF SERVICE ON THE BOARD, NOR SHALL ANY EMPLOYEE OF THE CORPORATION BE ELIGIBLE TO BE A BOARD MEMBER WHILE SO EMPLOYED.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Section B, Line 15a
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE CONTRACTUAL AGREEMENT FOR THE CEO, OFFICERS, AND KEY EMPLOYEES THAT DEFINES THE TERMS, CONDITIONS, AND COMPENSATION LEVEL IS DETERMINED USING REVIEW BY OUTSIDE LEGAL COUNSEL, ANALYSIS OF COMPARABLE AGENCY DATA, AND GOVERNING BOARD REVIEW. DELIBERATION AND DECISION IS RECORDED IN THE BOARD MINUTES.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Section C, Line 19
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: ALL GOVERNING DOCUMENTS, POLICIES, AND FINANCIAL REPORTS ARE MADE AVAILABLE TO THE PUBLIC UPON REQUEST.
